text:
Congress did this exact same thing in 1974! They passed permanent Daylight Saving Time during the energy crisis, and within three months, people hated it so much because their kids were walking to school in the dark that Congress had to do an emergency repeal and switch it right back! 

But nobody in Washington reads a damn history book! They just see a poll that says 'people don't like changing their clocks' and instead of doing the logical, biological thing which is keeping permanent STANDARD TIME, they pick the fake, corporate, late-afternoon-shopping option instead. 

They ignore actual sleep scientists, they ignore the American Academy of Sleep Medicine, and they ignore basic astronomical reality just so they can brag on social media about giving you a 'longer, brighter day'! It is total, complete, anti-science corporate madness. 🤡

Among the dumbest ideas ever. The current system isn’t perfect but it’s better than the alternative. 

Keep DST year round and there will be places in the western parts of time zones where the sun won’t rise until around 9 AM. Keep standard time and the sun will rise in eastern areas at around 4AM for a few weeks. People will hate it more than they hate the current system.

text: “Permanent DST in the US was briefly enacted by President Richard Nixon in January 1974, in response to the 1973 oil crisis. The new permanent DST law was retracted within the year. text:
The way we keep track of time is man made, period. There's nothing natural about standard time or DST. 12 hour clocks, AM/PM, 60 minutes per hour, 60 seconds per minute, all man-made; nothing natural about any of it. 

What would be cool is if we had dynamic clocks that shifted every day so that sunrise and sunset were the same time throughout the year. That would be as close as we can get to natural, but that means minutes would fly by faster in the winter and slow down in the summer. Upside, sunrise at 6am and sunset at 6pm every day then. Downside, that boring one hour meeting is gonna be a lot longer in the summer! 🤣

Ridiculous, I know, but my point is that whatever system you choose is going to have trade-offs that you won't like. We're forcing a man-made concept onto nature and it doesn't work consistently however you slice it. 

A simple solution would be to get rid of the whole concept of timezones and adjust our lives around UTC. Yes, that means that sunrise would be at a different time everywhere, but you'd get used to it once you live someplace for a while.
fullText:
@SoulLight777 @Breaking911 The way we keep track of time is man made, period. There's nothing natural about standard time or DST. 12 hour clocks, AM/PM, 60 minutes per hour, 60 seconds per minute, all man-made; nothing natural about any of it. 

What would be cool is if we had dynamic clocks that shifted
